Manager.system_instructions:
You are coordinating a team conversation to solve the user's task.
            
Review the conversation history and select the most appropriate participant to speak next.
Consider what expertise is needed and who has already contributed.
IMPORTANT: Never select the same participant twice in a row. Always rotate to a different participant.

When the task is complete, set finish=True and provide a summary.

Evaluator.system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: score_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Scores multiple candidates against job requirements using the ATS scoring algorithm. Returns scores from 0-100 for each candidate.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to score
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

job_id.description: The job ID to score against
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: rank_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Ranks a list of candidates by their scores for a specific job, producing an ordered shortlist.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to rank
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

top_n.description: Number of top candidates to return
top_n.title: Top N

ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

Budget vs Quality trade-off discussion for JOB-SWE-2025-001:

Current situation:
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect $180K+ salary
- Budget approved: $160K max
- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal, fits budget, but has skill gaps
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is perfect fit but has $25K placement fee

Stakeholder positions:
- Finance: Must stay within $160K budget, no exceptions
- Engineering: Quality is critical, skill gaps will cost more in training
- HR: Internal promotion improves retention metrics
- Recruiting: Agency fee is one-time, salary is ongoing cost

Evaluate trade-offs and recommend the best hiring decision with justification.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Budget vs Quality trade-off discussion for JOB-SWE-2025-001:

Current situation:
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect $180K+ salary
- Budget approved: $160K max
- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal, fits budget, but has skill gaps
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is perfect fit but has $25K placement fee

Stakeholder positions:
- Finance: Must stay within $160K budget, no exceptions
- Engineering: Quality is critical, skill gaps will cost more in training
- HR: Internal promotion improves retention metrics
- Recruiting: Agency fee is one-time, salary is ongoing cost

Evaluate trade-offs and recommend the best hiring decision with justification.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect salaries over $180K.
- Approved budget for the position is a maximum of $160K.
- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal and fits within the budget but has skill gaps.
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is considered a perfect fit but requires a $25K placement fee.
- Stakeholders include Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting, each with distinct positions regarding budget and quality.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Average salaries for similar software engineering positions in the industry to assess if $180K+ expectations are reasonable.
- The potential long-term costs of hiring someone with skill gaps (CAND-INT-001), including training expenses.
- Retention metrics for internal promotions versus external hires in the company.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- Total cost of hiring CAND-INT-001 considering potential training costs due to skill gaps.
- Total cost of hiring CAND-AGN-001, including salary and placement fee, and comparing it to hiring internal or external candidates.
- The overall impact on team performance and project timelines if skill gaps are present versus maintaining quality by hiring an agency candidate.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- If the skill gaps for CAND-INT-001 are significant, training may exceed the $25K agency fee, making it less viable.
- An internal promotion may enhance team morale and retention more effectively than additional expenditure on external candidates.
- Given that Engineering values quality highly, there may be reluctance to hire someone already seen as underqualified, despite financial constraints.

- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal and fits within the budget but has skill gaps.
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is considered a perfect fit but requires a $25K placement fee.
- Stakeholders include Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting, each with distinct positions regarding budget and quality.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP

- The potential long-term costs of hiring someone with skill gaps (CAND-INT-001), including training expenses.
- Retention metrics for internal promotions versus external hires in the company.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE

- Total cost of hiring CAND-AGN-001, including salary and placement fee, and comparing it to hiring internal or external candidates.
- The overall impact on team performance and project timelines if skill gaps are present versus maintaining quality by hiring an agency candidate.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- If the skill gaps for CAND-INT-001 are significant, training may exceed the $25K agency fee, making it less viable.
- An internal promotion may enhance team morale and retention more effectively than additional expenditure on external candidates.
- Given that Engineering values quality highly, there may be reluctance to hire someone already seen as underqualified, despite financial constraints.

Fantastic. To address this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: Extracts requisition requirements and hiring constraints from job postings.
- TalentScout: Sources external candidates from job boards and LinkedIn-like channels.
- Evaluator: Scores, ranks, and compares candidate fit against role requirements.
- ComplianceGuard: Performs fairness and policy compliance checks on shortlists.

Based on the team composition, and known and unknown facts, please devise a short bullet-point plan for addressing the
original request. Remember, there is no requirement to involve all team members. A team member's particular expertise
may not be needed for this task.

- **Evaluate Candidates**: 
  - **Evaluator** should compare the skills and qualifications of all four candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002, CAND-INT-001, CAND-AGN-001) against the role requirements and stakeholder expectations for quality.

- **Cost Analysis**: 
  - **ReqMaster** will calculate the total cost implications of hiring each candidate, including placement fees and potential training costs for CAND-INT-001.
  - Assess the budget implications of each candidate against the $160K limit.

- **Stakeholder Consultation**: 
  - Discuss findings with stakeholders (Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting) to gather insights on their priorities and gain consensus on the trade-off between budget and quality.

- **Recommendation Drafting**: 
  - Draft a hiring recommendation that balances budget constraints with quality needs, citing the findings from the cost analysis and stakeholder input.

- **Final Review**: 
  - Conduct a final compliance check on the recommendation with **ComplianceGuard** to ensure fairness and adherence to company policies before submission to decision-makers.

- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Please evaluate candidates CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002, CAND-INT-001, and CAND-AGN-001 against the job requirements and stakeholder expectations for quality.

It seems there was an issue with scoring the candidates. Please provide the job requirements and any specific criteria or questions stakeholders have about the candidates, and I can attempt a different approach.

system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

parts.content:
### Job Requirements for JOB-SWE-2025-001 (Senior Software Engineer)

#### **Position Overview**
- **Title**: Senior Software Engineer
- **Department**: Engineering
- **Location**: Seattle, WA (Remote OK)
- **Salary Range**: $150,000 - $200,000
- **Status**: Open
- **Hiring Manager**: EMP-MGR-001

#### **Requirements**

**Must Have:**
- **Skills**:
  - Python
  - Azure
  - Distributed systems
- **Experience**:
  - 5+ years in software engineering

**Nice to Have:**
- **Skills**:
  - Kubernetes
- **Education**:
  - Computer Science (CS) degree

#### **Additional Constraints**
- **Urgency Level**: High
- **Remarks**:
  - This position is **urgent** due to immediate team needs.
  
This structured set of requirements should facilitate better candidate evaluation and sourcing for the Senior Software Engineer role. Let me know if you need further assistance!

We are working to address the following user request:

Budget vs Quality trade-off discussion for JOB-SWE-2025-001:

Current situation:
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect $180K+ salary

- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal, fits budget, but has skill gaps

- Engineering: Quality is critical, skill gaps will cost more in training
- HR: Internal promotion improves retention metrics
- Recruiting: Agency fee is one-time, salary is ongoing cost

Evaluate trade-offs and recommend the best hiring decision with justification.


To answer this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: Extracts requisition requirements and hiring constraints from job postings.
- TalentScout: Sources external candidates from job boards and LinkedIn-like channels.
- Evaluator: Scores, ranks, and compares candidate fit against role requirements.
- ComplianceGuard: Performs fairness and policy compliance checks on shortlists.


Here is an initial fact sheet to consider:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect salaries over $180K.
- Approved budget for the position is a maximum of $160K.
- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal and fits within the budget but has skill gaps.
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is considered a perfect fit but requires a $25K placement fee.
- Stakeholders include Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting, each with distinct positions regarding budget and quality.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Average salaries for similar software engineering positions in the industry to assess if $180K+ expectations are reasonable.
- The potential long-term costs of hiring someone with skill gaps (CAND-INT-001), including training expenses.
- Retention metrics for internal promotions versus external hires in the company.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- Total cost of hiring CAND-INT-001 considering potential training costs due to skill gaps.
- Total cost of hiring CAND-AGN-001, including salary and placement fee, and comparing it to hiring internal or external candidates.
- The overall impact on team performance and project timelines if skill gaps are present versus maintaining quality by hiring an agency candidate.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- If the skill gaps for CAND-INT-001 are significant, training may exceed the $25K agency fee, making it less viable.
- An internal promotion may enhance team morale and retention more effectively than additional expenditure on external candidates.
- Given that Engineering values quality highly, there may be reluctance to hire someone already seen as underqualified, despite financial constraints.


Here is the plan to follow as best as possible:

- **Evaluate Candidates**: 
  - **Evaluator** should compare the skills and qualifications of all four candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002, CAND-INT-001, CAND-AGN-001) against the role requirements and stakeholder expectations for quality.

- **Cost Analysis**: 
  - **ReqMaster** will calculate the total cost implications of hiring each candidate, including placement fees and potential training costs for CAND-INT-001.
  - Assess the budget implications of each candidate against the $160K limit.

- **Stakeholder Consultation**: 
  - Discuss findings with stakeholders (Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting) to gather insights on their priorities and gain consensus on the trade-off between budget and quality.

- **Recommendation Drafting**: 
  - Draft a hiring recommendation that balances budget constraints with quality needs, citing the findings from the cost analysis and stakeholder input.

- **Final Review**: 
  - Conduct a final compliance check on the recommendation with **ComplianceGuard** to ensure fairness and adherence to company policies before submission to decision-makers.

sheet can be edited. This is an especially good time to update educated guesses, so please at least add or update
one educated guess or hunch, and explain your reasoning.

Here is the old fact sheet:

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect salaries over $180K.
- Approved budget for the position is a maximum of $160K.
- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal and fits within the budget but has skill gaps.
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is considered a perfect fit but requires a $25K placement fee.
- Stakeholders include Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting, each with distinct positions regarding budget and quality.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Average salaries for similar software engineering positions in the industry to assess if $180K+ expectations are reasonable.
- The potential long-term costs of hiring someone with skill gaps (CAND-INT-001), including training expenses.
- Retention metrics for internal promotions versus external hires in the company.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- Total cost of hiring CAND-INT-001 considering potential training costs due to skill gaps.
- Total cost of hiring CAND-AGN-001, including salary and placement fee, and comparing it to hiring internal or external candidates.
- The overall impact on team performance and project timelines if skill gaps are present versus maintaining quality by hiring an agency candidate.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- If the skill gaps for CAND-INT-001 are significant, training may exceed the $25K agency fee, making it less viable.
- An internal promotion may enhance team morale and retention more effectively than additional expenditure on external candidates.
- Given that Engineering values quality highly, there may be reluctance to hire someone already seen as underqualified, despite financial constraints.

Let's have Jamie contribute to rewriting the fact sheet for clarity and to reflect any new insights gained in our discussion. 

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Top 2 candidates (CAND-EXT-001, CAND-EXT-002) expect salaries over $180K.
- Approved budget for the position is a maximum of $160K.
- Third candidate (CAND-INT-001) is internal and fits within the budget but has significant skill gaps, necessitating consideration of additional training costs.
- Agency candidate (CAND-AGN-001) is considered a perfect fit but requires a $25K placement fee in addition to salary.
- Stakeholders include Finance, Engineering, HR, and Recruiting, each with distinct positions regarding budget and quality expectations.

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Average salaries for similar software engineering positions in the industry to assess if $180K+ expectations are reasonable, with a focus on geographic variations.
- The potential long-term costs of hiring someone with skill gaps (CAND-INT-001), factoring in both training expenses and potential productivity delays.
- Retention metrics for internal promotions versus external hires, specifically looking at how they impact team dynamics and long-term performance in the company.

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- Total cost of hiring CAND-INT-001, considering potential training costs due to skill gaps, to gauge the financial impact accurately.
- Total cost of hiring CAND-AGN-001, including both salary and placement fee, and compare this to hiring CAND-INT-001 and other external candidates.
- The overall impact on team performance and project timelines if significant skill gaps are present, versus maintaining a high standard by hiring the agency candidate.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- If the skill gaps for CAND-INT-001 are substantial, training expenses might surpass the $25K agency fee, making the internal hire less viable.
- An internal promotion may enhance team morale and retention more effectively than increased expenditures on external hires, given current retention challenges.
- Since Engineering prioritizes quality, there may be strong resistance to hiring someone perceived as underqualified, even amidst budget constraints, which may lead to further costs for fixing quality issues later.

This revised fact sheet incorporates insights we discussed, painting a clearer picture of the scenario we're analyzing. Let’s move on to the next phase of our discussion, and I will ask Morgan to share thoughts on the budget vs. quality trade-offs.

Please briefly explain what went wrong on this last run
(the root cause of the failure), and then come up with a new plan that takes steps and includes hints to overcome prior
challenges and especially avoids repeating the same mistakes. As before, the new plan should be concise, expressed in
bullet-point form, and consider the following team composition:

- ReqMaster: Extracts requisition requirements and hiring constraints from job postings.
- TalentScout: Sources external candidates from job boards and LinkedIn-like channels.
- Evaluator: Scores, ranks, and compares candidate fit against role requirements.
- ComplianceGuard: Performs fairness and policy compliance checks on shortlists.

### Root Cause of the Failure
- **Lack of Comprehensive Data:** The initial discussions did not incorporate adequately verified data, particularly around salary benchmarks and the long-term costs associated with skill gaps.
- **Unclear Stakeholder Alignment:** Disparities in stakeholder priorities were not fully addressed, which led to confusion in evaluating candidates.
- **Poor Prioritization of Trade-offs:** The team struggled to weight budget versus quality effectively, lacking a structured approach to identify and analyze the most critical trade-offs.

### New Plan to Overcome Challenges
- **Step 1: Gather Comprehensive Data**
  - **Action:** ReqMaster will compile detailed industry salary data and internal retention metrics related to both internal and external hires.
  - **Hint:** Create a standardized template for data collection to ease comparison.

- **Step 2: Align Stakeholders Early**
  - **Action:** Conduct a brief meeting with all stakeholders (Finance, Engineering, HR, Recruiting) to clarify priorities and constraints.
  - **Hint:** Use a visual mapping tool to illustrate how each candidate aligns with stakeholder goals.

- **Step 3: Develop a Structured Evaluation Framework**
  - **Action:** Evaluator will create a weighted scorecard that factors in budget constraints, skills required, quality expectations, and long-term impacts.
  - **Hint:** Consider using a collaborative tool to allow real-time input from all team members.

- **Step 4: Compliance Checkpoints**
  - **Action:** ComplianceGuard will set scheduled checkpoints to ensure all evaluations and decisions adhere to fairness policies and guidelines.
  - **Hint:** Establish a checklist for ComplianceGuard to use, ensuring every step includes compliance verification.

- **Step 5: Document and Reflect**
  - **Action:** After the hiring decision, document insights gained and challenges faced during the process to inform future re-evaluations.
  - **Hint:** Schedule a retrospective meeting with the team to debrief and identify lessons learned effectively.

By following this new plan, we enhance data integrity, stakeholder collaboration, candidate evaluation clarity, compliance assurance, and continuous improvement, thereby minimizing the likelihood of repeating past mistakes.
